reference.title: GitHub Actions: CI/CD Publishing and Workflow Examples
reference.shortdesc: Production-ready GitHub Actions workflows for PR checks, multi-variant builds, GitHub Pages deployment, and release packaging using the official dita-ot-action.

section.title: The official dita-ot-action
section.p: DITA-OT documents using GitHub Actions and notes that the project provides an official action (as of 3.6.1) to build docs as part of CI/CD. Steps run on the same runner, so build outputs can be archived or published by later steps.
The dita-ot-action repo README states the action installs DITA-OT and specified plug-ins and builds outputs like PDF or HTML.

section.title: PR checks: build HTML and upload as artifact
section.codeblock:
name: DITA PR build
on: pull_request:
jobs: build: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
- name: Build HTML5 uses: dita-ot/dita-ot-action@master with: input: dita/maps/book.ditamap transtype: html5 output-path: out/html5
- name: Upload HTML artifact uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4 with: name: html5 path: out/html5

section.title: Multi-variant builds: audience/platform filters as matrix
Deploy HTML to GitHub Pages on main
section.codeblock:
name: Publish docs to Pages
on: push: branches: [ "main" ]
permissions: contents: read pages: write id-token: write
concurrency: group: "pages" cancel-in-progress: true
jobs: build: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4
- name: Build HTML5 site uses: dita-ot/dita-ot-action@master with: input: dita/maps/book.ditamap transtype: html5 output-path: out/site
- name: Upload Pages artifact uses: actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 with: path: out/site
deploy: needs: build runs-on: ubuntu-latest environment: name: github-pages url: ${{ steps.deployment.outputs.page_url }} steps: - id: deployment uses: actions/deploy-pages@v4

section.title: Release builds: attach outputs to GitHub Releases
section.p: At release time you may want:
Use actions/upload-artifact for PRs and softprops/action-gh-release (or similar) for release tagging.
